title: GitHub Profile, Repository Health, and Verifiable Work · Hiring Decision Lens: Reproducible Proof
summary: A profile has a dense contribution graph and many forked tutorials, but reviewers cannot find three relevant, runnable, maintained, or honestly attributed examples. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the reproducible proof increment in selective GitHub evidence route. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.
Show license, contribution, conduct, support, security, issue, dependency, release, and ownership signals appropriate to the project.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Reproducible Proof · Theory Reproducible Proof
steps.instruction:
theory case — A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Reproducible Proof contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Reproducible Proof · Theory Reproducible Proof.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route. For Predict Reproducible Proof · Theory Reproducible Proof, keep Reproducible Proof connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The theory variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. content.text: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A dense contribution graph and ten pinned tutorials hide the three relevant projects, setup path, tests, current status, contribution boundary, and limitations. The worked example variation 3 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route. A defensible solution applies Reproducible Proof, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to selective GitHub evidence route.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A passing historical workflow badge proves the project works now. This fails the Reproducible Proof contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CareerOneStop Job Search (U.S. Department of Labor sponsored guidance reviewed 2026-07-15) — Controls the connected search flow across planning, employer research, networking, applications, portfolios, interviews, background checks, and negotiation.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify one clean setup, core behavior, changed case, failure, and current dependency or environment boundary from the public route.

stimulus.title: Reproducible Proof failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed

lines.label: required

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Reproducible Proof, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
